agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"E-commerce platform managing the product catalog, shopping cart, checkout, promotions, and post-purchase lifecycle. The rules govern inventory reservation timing, discount stacking policy, order immutability after payment, and refund eligibility windows.","bounded_context":"Order & Checkout bounded context, with touchpoints into Inventory, Promotions/Pricing, and Returns/Refunds contexts.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Cart","definition":"A transient collection of items a customer intends to purchase. Adding items to a cart does NOT reserve inventory."},{"term":"Checkout","definition":"The process initiated when a customer commits to purchasing cart contents. Triggers inventory reservation."},{"term":"Inventory Reservation","definition":"A time-limited hold on inventory units that begins at checkout initiation and expires after 15 minutes if payment is not completed."},{"term":"Abandoned Checkout","definition":"A checkout session where payment is not completed within 15 minutes of initiation, causing all associated inventory reservations to be released."},{"term":"Promotion","definition":"A pricing mechanism applied to a product (e.g., sale price, percentage off) managed by the business. A product may have at most one active promotion at any time."},{"term":"Discount Code","definition":"A customer-supplied code that grants a price reduction. Mutually exclusive with promotional pricing on the same order."},{"term":"Payment Confirmed","definition":"The terminal, irreversible state of an order indicating successful payment capture. After this point, the order is immutable."},{"term":"Zero Inventory","definition":"A product state where available stock is zero. Such products are unpurchasable regardless of catalog listing status."},{"term":"Refund Window","definition":"A 30-day period beginning at delivery date during which a customer may request a refund."},{"term":"Delivery Date","definition":"The date on which the order is recorded as delivered to the customer; starts the refund eligibility window."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Adding items to a cart does not reserve inventory. Inventory remains available to all customers until checkout begins.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If cart additions reserved inventory, phantom holds would corrupt available stock counts system-wide, leading to false out-of-stock for other customers.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: After a customer adds N units of product P to their cart, the available inventory count for P is unchanged."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Inventory is reserved when checkout begins. The reserved quantity is deducted from available inventory.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Without reservation at checkout, two customers could simultaneously pay for the last unit, causing overselling — a data consistency violation.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: When checkout is initiated for N units of product P, available_inventory(P) decreases by N and reserved_inventory(P) increases by N."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"If payment is not completed within 15 minutes of checkout initiation, the reservation is released and inventory is returned to available stock.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 15-minute window is a business policy tunable by operations; the invariant is that abandoned checkouts MUST eventually release reservations, but the specific timeout is configurable.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: A checkout initiated at T with no payment by T+15m results in reserved_inventory decreasing and available_inventory increasing by the reserved quantity."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A product may have at most one active promotion at any given time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Multiple simultaneous promotions on a single product create ambiguity in which price to display and charge, corrupting pricing integrity.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: For any product P at any point in time, COUNT(active_promotions(P)) <= 1."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Discount codes and promotional pricing are mutually exclusive per order. Only one discount mechanism applies.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing stacking of discount codes on top of promotional pricing can result in below-cost sales and revenue leakage — a financial integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: For any order, if a promotional price is applied, no discount code reduction is applied, and vice versa."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Orders cannot be modified after payment is confirmed.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Post-payment modification breaks financial reconciliation, inventory accuracy, and audit trail integrity. The order record must be immutable once payment is captured.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: Any attempt to modify an order in state PAYMENT_CONFIRMED returns an error and the order remains unchanged."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"A product with zero available inventory cannot be purchased, even if still visible in the catalog.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing purchase of zero-inventory items leads to overselling, broken fulfillment promises, and negative customer experience — a physical constraint.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: Initiating checkout for a product where available_inventory == 0 is rejected with an out-of-stock error."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"Refund requests must be submitted within 30 days of the recorded delivery date.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 30-day window is a business policy that could be extended by management for goodwill or regulatory compliance; the core invariant is that a refund window exists and is enforced.","testable_assertion":"ASSERT: A refund request submitted more than 30 days after delivery_date is rejected. A request submitted within 30 days is accepted for processing."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer has items in their cart","when":"They have not initiated checkout","then":"Available inventory for those items remains unchanged and other customers can still purchase them"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer initiates checkout with N units of product P in their cart","when":"Product P has N or more units of available inventory","then":"N units are reserved, available inventory decreases by N, and the customer proceeds to payment"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A customer initiates checkout with N units of product P","when":"Product P has fewer than N units of available inventory","then":"Checkout is rejected with an insufficient-inventory error and no reservation is created"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A checkout was initiated and inventory reserved","when":"15 minutes elapse without payment completion","then":"The reservation is released, available inventory is restored by the reserved quantity, and the checkout session is marked as abandoned"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A checkout was initiated and inventory reserved","when":"Payment is completed within 15 minutes","then":"The reservation converts to a confirmed allocation, the order transitions to PAYMENT_CONFIRMED, and the reservation timer is cancelled"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A product already has an active promotion","when":"An attempt is made to add a second promotion to the same product","then":"The attempt is rejected and the existing promotion remains unchanged"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A customer's cart contains a product with an active promotional price","when":"The customer also applies a discount code at checkout","then":"Only one discount mechanism is applied; the system either rejects the discount code or removes the promotional pricing, applying whichever single mechanism the business policy dictates"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"An order is in PAYMENT_CONFIRMED state","when":"Any modification is attempted (item change, quantity change, address change)","then":"The modification is rejected and the order remains exactly as it was at payment confirmation"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A product has zero available inventory but is still listed in the catalog","when":"A customer attempts to add it to cart and check out","then":"The checkout (or add-to-cart-and-checkout flow) is blocked with an out-of-stock error; no reservation is created"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An order was delivered on date D","when":"A customer requests a refund on or before D+30 days","then":"The refund request is accepted and enters the refund processing workflow"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An order was delivered on date D","when":"A customer requests a refund after D+30 days","then":"The refund request is rejected with a message indicating the refund window has expired"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"Two customers attempt to purchase the last unit of a product simultaneously","when":"Both initiate checkout at nearly the same time","then":"Only one checkout successfully reserves the unit; the other is rejected with an out-of-stock error (no overselling)"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Customer adds item to cart, item goes to zero inventory before checkout","expected_behavior":"Checkout initiation fails with out-of-stock error. Cart may still show the item but purchase is blocked."},{"scenario":"Reservation timer fires at exactly the moment payment is submitted","expected_behavior":"System must handle race condition atomically — either payment succeeds and cancels the timer, or timer fires first and payment fails. Must not result in both a confirmed order AND released inventory."},{"scenario":"Promotion expires while customer is in checkout","expected_behavior":"The price locked at checkout initiation should apply (or the system re-validates pricing at payment confirmation). Business must define which — but the order must not have an inconsistent price."},{"scenario":"Discount code is applied to an order where some items have promotions and others do not","expected_behavior":"Per BR-5, discount code and promotional pricing are mutually exclusive per order. Clarify: does the code apply only to non-promoted items, or is the entire code rejected? Current spec says one mechanism per order."},{"scenario":"Customer requests refund on an item from a multi-item order","expected_behavior":"Partial refund eligibility within the 30-day window needs clarification — spec says 'orders' but real-world is often per-item."},{"scenario":"Product inventory reaches zero between reservation creation and payment confirmation","expected_behavior":"The reservation already secured the units, so payment should succeed. The reservation is the mechanism that prevents this race."},{"scenario":"Customer abandons checkout at 14:59 and resubmits payment at 15:01","expected_behavior":"Reservation is released at 15:00. Payment at 15:01 must fail and customer must re-initiate checkout (subject to inventory availability)."},{"scenario":"Admin attempts to add a promotion to a product that currently has a future-dated promotion scheduled","expected_behavior":"Depends on whether 'active' means currently-live or includes scheduled. If scheduled counts, it is rejected per BR-4."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Inventory reservation timing directly impacts conversion rate and revenue. Too-long reservations cause phantom stock-outs; too-short ones cause checkout failures. The mutual exclusivity of discounts prevents margin erosion from stacking. The 30-day refund window bounds financial liability.","operational":"The 15-minute reservation window creates a background process requirement (timer/scheduler) that must be highly reliable. Promotion uniqueness constraint simplifies pricing engine logic. Order immutability after payment simplifies fulfillment and audit workflows.","user":"Customers may be frustrated if items in their cart become unavailable before checkout. The discount mutual exclusivity may surprise customers who expect to stack a code on a sale item. Clear messaging is critical at these enforcement points.","risk":"Primary risks: (1) Race conditions in inventory reservation could cause overselling — requires transactional guarantees. (2) Timer reliability for reservation release — a stuck timer permanently locks inventory. (3) Ambiguity in discount precedence (code vs. promotion) could lead to customer complaints if not clearly communicated.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Experience inventory availability changes between cart and checkout; cannot combine discounts; have a fixed refund window. Clear UX messaging is essential."},{"group":"Product/Merchandising Team","impact":"Must manage promotions knowing only one can be active per product. Need tooling to view and manage active promotions and prevent conflicts."},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"Order immutability post-payment simplifies reconciliation. Refund window bounds liability exposure. Discount exclusivity prevents unintended margin loss."},{"group":"Warehouse/Fulfillment","impact":"Reservation-based inventory ensures allocated items are available for picking. No post-payment order changes means stable pick lists."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Must handle complaints about: items going out of stock between cart and checkout, rejected discount+promotion combinations, and refund window denials."},{"group":"Engineering/Platform Team","impact":"Must implement atomic inventory reservation, reliable timeout mechanisms, and race-condition-safe checkout flows. Non-trivial distributed systems concerns."}]},"confidence":{"level":82,"reasoning":"The specification is well-articulated with clear, non-contradictory rules. All eight stated rules are unambiguous in their intent. Confidence is not higher because several practical edge cases (discount precedence, partial refunds, promotion scheduling semantics) require additional product decisions that are not addressed in the spec but do not constitute contradictions or critical missing context — they are refinements needed during implementation.","high_confidence_areas":["Inventory is not reserved at cart-add time","15-minute reservation timeout and release","One active promotion per product","Order immutability after payment confirmation","Zero-inventory products cannot be purchased","30-day refund window from delivery"],"low_confidence_areas":["Which discount mechanism takes precedence when both a promotion and discount code could apply — spec says only one applies but does not define selection logic or UX behavior","Whether 'active promotion' includes future-scheduled promotions or only currently-live ones","Whether refund eligibility is per-order or per-item for multi-item orders","Whether the cart should proactively warn customers about low/zero inventory before checkout","Price locking semantics — is the price locked at checkout initiation or re-validated at payment confirmation"],"assumptions":["The 15-minute timeout is measured from checkout initiation, not from the last user interaction","Inventory reservation is atomic and transactional to prevent overselling","The refund window is calculated from the delivery date, not the ship date or order date","Order immutability means no changes to line items, quantities, or pricing — address changes for unshipped orders may be a separate policy not covered here"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"E-commerce platform managing the product catalog, shopping cart, checkout, promotions, and post-purchase lifecycle. The rules govern inventory reservation timing, discount stacking policy, order immutability after payment, and refund eligibility windows.","bounded_context":"Order & Checkout bounded context, with touchpoints into Inventory, Promotions/Pricing, and Returns/Refunds contexts.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Cart","definition":"A transient col

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this e-commerce domain scenario carefully.

The rules are clear and well-defined. Let me classify each:

1. Cart doesn't reserve inventory - policy
2. Checkout reserves inventory - invariant (state integrity)
3. 15-min abandonment releases reservation - policy (time window)
4. Max one active promotion per product - invariant (data consistency)
5. Discount codes can't combine with promotional pricing - invariant (data consistency)
6. Orders immutable after payment confirmed - invariant (state integrity)


7. Zero inventory blocks purchases regardless of listing status - invariant (state integrity)
8. Refunds available within 30 days of delivery - policy (time window)

The specification is internally consistent with no contradictions or ambiguities. I'm ready to generate the JSON output with moderate-high confidence.

A request submitted within 30 days is accepted for processing."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer has items in their cart","when":"They have not initiated checkout","then":"Available inventory for those items remains unchanged and other customers can still purchase them"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer initiates checkout with N units of product P in their cart","when":"Product P has N or more units of available inventory","then":"N units are reserved, available inventory decreases by N, and the customer proceeds to payment"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A customer initiates checkout with N units of product P","when":"Product P has fewer than N units of available inventory","then":"Checkout is rejected with an insufficient-inventory error and no reservation is created"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A checkout was initiated and inventory reserved","when":"15 minutes elapse without payment completion","then":"The reservation is released, available inventory is restored by the reserved quantity, and the checkout session is marked as abandoned"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A checkout was initiated and inventory reserved","when":"Payment is completed within 15 minutes","then":"The reservation converts to a confirmed allocation, the order transitions to PAYMENT_CONFIRMED, and the reservation timer is cancelled"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A product already has an active promotion","when":"An attempt is made to add a second promotion to the same product","then":"The attempt is rejected and the existing promotion remains unchanged"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A customer's cart contains a product with an active promotional price","when":"The customer also applies a discount code at checkout","then":"Only one discount mechanism is applied; the system either rejects the discount code or removes the promotional pricing, applying whichever single mechanism the business policy dictates"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"An order is in PAYMENT_CONFIRMED state","when":"Any modification is attempted (item change, quantity change, address change)","then":"The modification is rejected and the order remains exactly as it was at payment confirmation"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A product has zero available inventory but is still listed in the catalog","when":"A customer attempts to add it to cart and check out","then":"The checkout (or add-to-cart-and-checkout flow) is blocked with an out-of-stock error; no reservation is created"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An order was delivered on date D","when":"A customer requests a refund on or before D+30 days","then":"The refund request is accepted and enters the refund processing workflow"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An order was delivered on date D","when":"A customer requests a refund after D+30 days","then":"The refund request is rejected with a message indicating the refund window has expired"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"Two customers attempt to purchase the last unit of a product simultaneously","when":"Both initiate checkout at nearly the same time","then":"Only one checkout successfully reserves the unit; the other is rejected with an out-of-stock error (no overselling)"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Customer adds item to cart, item goes to zero inventory before checkout","expected_behavior":"Checkout initiation fails with out-of-stock error. Cart may still show the item but purchase is blocked."},{"scenario":"Reservation timer fires at exactly the moment payment is submitted","expected_behavior":"System must handle race condition atomically — either payment succeeds and cancels the timer, or timer fires first and payment fails.
